steps.title: Keep one main verb
steps.content: The sentence's main subject is "Antiquarians," and the main verb phrase is "might instead procure." The blank should describe those antiquarians, not create another main verb before "might procure." A participle can modify the noun while leaving the modal verb as the sentence's main action.

steps.title: Reject extra finite verbs
steps.content: A, C, and D are finite verb forms. Each would make "Antiquarians" appear to have a separate main verb before "might instead procure," producing an uncoordinated two-verb structure. The participle form correctly describes antiquarians who want to read an extant work. The answer is B.
